notes: ok so the excavator just went through AH wait no not through a pipe, NEAR the pipe, the storm water pipe near grid F6. didn't hit it but it was close, maybe 100 mils away
the plaster is applied in the lounge room looks terrible honestly theres like 3 or 4 patches where you can see the joints showing through. gonna need to be sanded back and recoated
too much slab waiting i mean two months waiting on the slab reinforcement from the supplier theyve been saying next week for three weeks now

the die electric the DIELECTRIC union for the hot water system hasnt been installed which means the copper and steel are touching directly gonna get corrosion there for sure. told the plumber to fix it
crane is down today, matenance maintenance, think its the slew ring bearings or something. mechanic reckons back up tomorrow
just measured the window opening on the north wall and its 10 mil out of square. within tollerance tolerance but only just. the window guys should be able to shim it

delivery of the I dont even know what theyre called those bracket things for the the uh facade cladding support brackets thats it. anyway they showed up and theyre the wrong size. supposed to be 150mm and these are 100mm. rejected the delivery sent them back

waterproof membrane on the bathroom floor looks good BUT they didnt take it up the wall high enough. code says 150mm above finished floor level and this looks like maybe 100 at best. flagged it, they're coming back
scaff guys are here erecting the scaffold on the east side of the building. should take em most of the day. 4 bays wide and going up to level 3 so thats about 9 meters high
the new apprentice is alright actually. hes keen, shows up on time, asks questions. been getting him to do the site cleanup and basic measurements. names Jake I think. or Jack. one of those

sorry about the background noise theres a jackhammer going right behind me. ANYWAY the point is the structural steel for the mezzanine floor has arrived and its being unloaded now. about 15 members total including the main beams and the secondary purlins
